name: Follow-up Call
description: Universal follow-up call script used after initial email outreach or a prior call where interest was shown but no meeting was booked. Goal: recap briefly, address any blockers, and advance to the next step (meeting or materials).

nodes.label: Reminder of context
nodes.script: Hi {contact_name}, this is [caller] from Corgi Insurance Services. I sent you a note about a week ago on GPU Residual Value Guaranty — I wanted to follow up with a quick call rather than another email. Is now an okay moment?

branches.condition: I saw it but not interested

nodes.label: 30-second recap
nodes.script: Quick recap: we provide insurance that puts a contractual floor on GPU hardware value at a future date — a Residual Value Guaranty. It's relevant for GPU operators protecting their balance sheet, lenders who want guaranteed collateral recovery, and reinsurers looking for a new RV program to write capacity into. Based on what {company} is doing, I thought [operator angle / lender angle / reinsurer angle] was the most relevant hook. Has anything shifted on your end since I wrote?

nodes.label: Bad time
nodes.script: Of course. When would be better? I have something specific I want to put in front of you — takes 60 seconds — and then I'll leave the decision to you.

nodes.label: Email not seen
nodes.script: No problem — let me give you the short version now. We provide Residual Value Guaranty insurance on GPU hardware — a put-style floor on hardware value at a specified future date. If the GPUs are worth less than the guaranteed percentage at policy expiry, the policy pays out the full guaranteed amount and we take title to the hardware. We're focused on GPU operators, lenders doing GPU-backed facilities, and reinsurers looking for capacity. Does any of those angles resonate for {company}?

branches.condition: yes / one of those is relevant

nodes.label: Saw email, passed
nodes.script: Totally fair. Before I let you go — what was the blocker? Was it the timing, the product didn't fit, or something else? I want to make sure I'm not wasting your time with the wrong angle.

branches.condition: don't own GPUs / don't do GPU lending

nodes.label: Interest confirmed — advance
nodes.script: Good to hear. The most useful next step is usually a 20-minute call with our founders — Isaac and Josh. They can walk through exactly how a policy would look for {company}, including indicative pricing and deal mechanics. No deck — just a working session on the numbers. Can we find a slot this week or next?

nodes.label: Needs more info
nodes.script: Happy to elaborate. What specifically would help — the product mechanics, how it fits into a financing structure, or pricing? I can either walk you through it now — takes 3 minutes — or I can send you a brief written overview and we can follow up once you've had a chance to look at it.

branches.condition: book a call with your founders

nodes.label: Wrong timing
nodes.script: Understood. I'd rather be relevant than persistent. Two quick questions: is there a specific event that would make this more timely — a financing close, a hardware purchase, a lender conversation? And is this a 'check back in 3 months' situation or more like 12+ months? That helps me calibrate.

nodes.label: Passed internally
nodes.script: Good to know — who did you pass it to? I want to make sure they have everything they need to evaluate it properly, and I'd rather reach out to them directly than have it sit in an inbox.

nodes.label: Final exit
nodes.script: Understood — I'll leave it here. If GPU residual value, hardware financing, or RV protection becomes relevant to {company} in the future, Corgi Insurance Services is the right call. Thanks for your time.

already_passed.trigger: we saw it / we passed / we decided not to

who_are_you_again.trigger: remind me who you are / what company is this
who_are_you_again.response: Corgi Insurance Services — we provide Residual Value Guaranty insurance for GPU hardware. Put-style floor on hardware value at a specified future date. We're the only insurance product built specifically for AI infrastructure. Does that ring a bell from the email?

exitStates.BOOK_MEETING: Log meeting, send calendar invite, trigger follow_up_2.hbs post-meeting
exitStates.SEND_FOLLOWUP_EMAIL: Send follow_up_1.hbs or follow_up_2.hbs depending on meeting status
exitStates.ROUTE_TO_AUDIENCE_SCRIPT: Transfer to script-gpu-operator.json node 5 / script-lender.json node 5 / script-reinsurer.json node 5 based on audience
exitStates.DISQUALIFY_AND_LOG: Mark as disqualified, log reason, remove from active sequence
exitStates.UPDATE_CONTACT_AND_RESTART: Update CRM with new contact, initiate appropriate audience script
